meta.title: Ambergris
meta.legacyPath: 睡着过项目组/2. 所有文章(11.20)[192篇]/4. P1 - Ambergris 龙涎香/

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.
What is it and where does it come from?

In the ancient world, the waxy grey substance we now refer to as ambergris was highly prized for its medicinal properties, and was widely used as a spice, which was believed to be an aphrodisiac when added to food or wine. Ambergris itself is pleasantly aromatic, especially when warmed, and it was also highly valued as a fixing agent in the making of perfume, since it enabled a scent to retain its fragrance for much longer than might otherwise have been possible. Most ambergris was found in the form of lumps floating on the surface of the sea, or washed up on the shores of tropical and temperate oceans. At one time, ambergris was worth its weight in gold, but there was much confusion about its origins.

Ambergris was known to the Arabs as 'ambar' and was originally called amber in the West in the Middle Ages. This eventually led to further confusion in the popular mind between ambergris and true amber, the mineral known to mineralogists as succinite, which is actually fossilised tree resin, and generally yellow in colour. Both substances were rare and costly, and both were associated with the sea, largely because for Europeans the most common source of amber was the shores of the Baltic. In Chapter 92 of Moby Dick, the American writer Herman Melville pours scorn on those who believed the two substances to be the same: ‘Though the word ambergris is but the French compound for grey amber, yet the two substances are quite distinct. For amber, though at times found on the sea-coast, is also dug up in some far-inland soils, whereas ambergris is never found except upon the sea. Besides, amber is a hard, transparent, brittle, odourless substance, used for mouth-pieces to pipes, for beads and ornaments; but ambergris is soft, waxy, and so highly fragrant that it is largely used in perfumery.'

Moby Dick was published in 1851, by which time the mystery of the origins of ambergris had been resolved by the scientific community. In 1783, the botanist Joseph Banks, who had accompanied Captain James Cook on his voyages of discovery in the Pacific, presented a paper to the Royal Society of London by the German physician Dr Franz Xavier Schwediawer in which it was conclusively proved that ambergris came from sperm whales. In this, he was confirming an observation made in the 13th century by the great Venetian traveller Marco Polo who, while on the island of Socotra in the Indian Ocean, had witnessed a sperm whale vomiting up ambergris. But whereas Marco Polo imagined that the whale had swallowed the lump in the depths of the sea, Schwediawer showed that the origin of the material was inside the whale itself.

The sperm whale is the largest of the odontocetes, or toothed whales. Males can grow up to 20 metres in length. Melville described the sperm whale as 'the king of whales', and his novel Moby Dick is based on the pursuit of one such creature. Sperm whales are renowned for their ability to dive to great depths, possibly as far as 3,000 metres below the surface, and for remaining underwater for periods of two hours or more in pursuit of their favourite prey, the giant squid.

It is from the problems the whales have in digesting the beaks of such creatures that ambergris has its origins. The beak is sharp and irritates the whale's lower intestine, which responds by producing a black, foul-smelling liquid. It is not clear to scientists whether this secretion should be considered a normal response by the whale's digestive system or a pathological one, but from time to time large quantities of the liquid are vomited up by the whale. Once outside the whale's body and exposed to air, the substance hardens, acquiring the waxy, greyish and pleasantly aromatic characteristics of ambergris. Often the beaks of squid are still found embedded in lumps of ambergris, some of which can weigh several hundred kilograms. Melville took some delight in contrasting the origins of ambergris with the high value placed upon it by refined society: 'Who would think, then, that such fine ladies and gentlemen should regale themselves with an essence found in the inglorious bowels of a sick whale!'

Sperm whales were ruthlessly pursued by commercial whalers in the 19th and 20th centuries. In 1963–64 alone, almost 30,000 individuals were killed, and only the imposition of a ban on the hunting of sperm whales in 1984 saved the species from extinction. Ambergris was by far the most valuable product to be extracted during the processing of the whales' carcasses, and over 90 per cent of the annual worldwide total was acquired in this way, as a by-product of commercial whaling. However, even before the ban on hunting sperm whales was imposed, the 1972 Marine Mammal Protection Act had prohibited trade in ambergris. Just as petroleum and plastic products were replacing other natural products of whaling, so ambergris was supplanted in the making of perfume by other materials, some natural and some synthetic in origin. Nevertheless, it is possible that, as sperm-whale populations recover to their former numbers in the wild, so the sight of lumps of ambergris washed ashore along the tide-line will once again become a familiar one to beach-combers the world over.
Classify the following statements as referring to
Drag and drop the correct option into the boxes for questions 1–6.
You may use any option more than once.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 1. 分类题（Questions 1–6：A = 龙涎香 only；B = 琥珀 only；C = 两者都符合；D = 两者都不符合）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：价格昂贵
答案：C（both，两者都符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “At one time, ambergris was worth its weight in gold”（龙涎香曾价值堪比黄金），以及 Paragraph 2 中 “Both substances were rare and costly”（两种物质都稀有且昂贵），可知龙涎香和琥珀均符合 “价格昂贵” 的描述，因此答案为 C。
题目翻译：一种食品调味剂
答案：A（ambergris only，仅龙涎香符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “the waxy grey substance we now refer to as ambergris... was widely used as a spice, which was believed to be an aphrodisiac when added to food or wine”，明确提到龙涎香被用作香料加入食物或酒中，即作为食品调味剂；文中未提及琥珀有 “食品调味” 的用途，因此答案为 A。
题目翻译：被用作货币
答案：D（neither，两者都不符合）
解析：全文未出现任何关于 “龙涎香” 或 “琥珀” 被当作 “货币” 使用的描述，既无肯定信息，也无相关暗示，因此答案为 D。
题目翻译：气味香甜
答案：A（ambergris only，仅龙涎香符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Ambergris itself is pleasantly aromatic, especially when warmed”（龙涎香气味宜人），以及 Paragraph 2 中 “amber is a hard, transparent, brittle, odourless substance”（琥珀无气味），可知仅龙涎香符合 “气味香甜” 的描述，因此答案为 A。
题目翻译：被赫尔曼・梅尔维尔提及
答案：C（both，两者都符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “In Chapter 92 of Moby Dick, the American writer Herman Melville pours scorn on those who believed the two substances to be the same... ‘Though the word ambergris... For amber, though at times found on the sea-coast...’”，梅尔维尔在《白鲸记》中同时提及龙涎香和琥珀，并对比两者的区别，因此答案为 C。
题目翻译：可被看穿（透明）
答案：B（amber only，仅琥珀符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “amber is a hard, transparent, brittle, odourless substance”（琥珀坚硬、透明、易碎、无气味），“transparent” 意为 “透明的”，即 “可被看穿”；文中描述龙涎香为 “soft, waxy”（柔软、蜡状），未提及 “透明” 属性，因此答案为 B。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：very expensive
题目翻译：价格昂贵
答案：C（both，两者都符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “At one time, ambergris was worth its weight in gold”（龙涎香曾价值堪比黄金），以及 Paragraph 2 中 “Both substances were rare and costly”（两种物质都稀有且昂贵），可知龙涎香和琥珀均符合 “价格昂贵” 的描述，因此答案为 C。

（2）题目 2：a food flavouring
题目翻译：一种食品调味剂
答案：A（ambergris only，仅龙涎香符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “the waxy grey substance we now refer to as ambergris... was widely used as a spice, which was believed to be an aphrodisiac when added to food or wine”，明确提到龙涎香被用作香料加入食物或酒中，即作为食品调味剂；文中未提及琥珀有 “食品调味” 的用途，因此答案为 A。

（3）题目 3：used as currency
题目翻译：被用作货币
答案：D（neither，两者都不符合）
解析：全文未出现任何关于 “龙涎香” 或 “琥珀” 被当作 “货币” 使用的描述，既无肯定信息，也无相关暗示，因此答案为 D。

（4）题目 4：sweet-smelling
题目翻译：气味香甜
答案：A（ambergris only，仅龙涎香符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Ambergris itself is pleasantly aromatic, especially when warmed”（龙涎香气味宜人），以及 Paragraph 2 中 “amber is a hard, transparent, brittle, odourless substance”（琥珀无气味），可知仅龙涎香符合 “气味香甜” 的描述，因此答案为 A。

（5）题目 5：referred to by Herman Melville
题目翻译：被赫尔曼・梅尔维尔提及
答案：C（both，两者都符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “In Chapter 92 of Moby Dick, the American writer Herman Melville pours scorn on those who believed the two substances to be the same... ‘Though the word ambergris... For amber, though at times found on the sea-coast...’”，梅尔维尔在《白鲸记》中同时提及龙涎香和琥珀，并对比两者的区别，因此答案为 C。

（6）题目 6：can be seen through
题目翻译：可被看穿（透明）
答案：B（amber only，仅琥珀符合）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “amber is a hard, transparent, brittle, odourless substance”（琥珀坚硬、透明、易碎、无气味），“transparent” 意为 “透明的”，即 “可被看穿”；文中描述龙涎香为 “soft, waxy”（柔软、蜡状），未提及 “透明” 属性，因此答案为 B。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
Ambergris is formed in whales because of problems digesting the
Black liquid is produced and is

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 流程图填空（Questions 7–9：NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：龙涎香形成于鲸体内，原因是鲸消化大王乌贼的_\___\__时遇到困难。
答案：beaks（喙部）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “It is from the problems the whales have in digesting the beaks of such creatures that ambergris has its origins”，“such creatures” 指前文提到的 “giant squid（大王乌贼）”，明确指出龙涎香的形成源于鲸消化大王乌贼 “喙部（beaks）” 的困难，因此答案为 “beaks”。
题目翻译：黑色液体产生后，时不时会被_\___\__。
答案：vomited up（吐出）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “from time to time large quantities of the liquid are vomited up by the whale”，可知鲸会 “时不时吐出（vomited up）” 这种黑色液体，因此答案为 “vomited up”。
题目翻译：这种液体接触空气后会_\___\__。
答案：hardens（变硬）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Once outside the whale's body and exposed to air, the substance hardens”，可知液体离开鲸体接触空气后会 “变硬（hardens）”，因此答案为 “hardens”。

items.text:
（1）题目 7：Ambergris is formed in whales because of problems digesting the \___\___ of giant squid.
题目翻译：龙涎香形成于鲸体内，原因是鲸消化大王乌贼的_\___\__时遇到困难。
答案：beaks（喙部）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “It is from the problems the whales have in digesting the beaks of such creatures that ambergris has its origins”，“such creatures” 指前文提到的 “giant squid（大王乌贼）”，明确指出龙涎香的形成源于鲸消化大王乌贼 “喙部（beaks）” 的困难，因此答案为 “beaks”。

（2）题目 8：Black liquid is produced and is from time to time \___\___.
题目翻译：黑色液体产生后，时不时会被_\___\__。
答案：vomited up（吐出）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “from time to time large quantities of the liquid are vomited up by the whale”，可知鲸会 “时不时吐出（vomited up）” 这种黑色液体，因此答案为 “vomited up”。

（3）题目 9：The liquid \___\___ on contact with the air.
题目翻译：这种液体接触空气后会_\___\__。
答案：hardens（变硬）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Once outside the whale's body and exposed to air, the substance hardens”，可知液体离开鲸体接触空气后会 “变硬（hardens）”，因此答案为 “hardens”。

children.text: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 10–13 on your answer sheet, write
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information on this
In the 20th century, most of the world's ambergris came from processing dead whales.
The value of ambergris has increased recently.
Ambergris remains an important ingredient in perfume.
New uses have recently been found for ambergris.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：20 世纪，全球大部分龙涎香来自对死亡鲸体的加工。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “over 90 per cent of the annual worldwide total was acquired in this way, as a by-product of commercial whaling”，“this way” 指前文的 “the processing of the whales’ carcasses（鲸尸加工）”，且该情况发生在 19-20 世纪商业捕鲸时期，90% 以上的占比说明 “大部分” 龙涎香来自死亡鲸体加工，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：龙涎香的价值近期有所上涨。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 6 仅提到龙涎香曾价值高昂，后因贸易禁令和替代品出现，其在香水制作中的地位下降，但未提及 “近期价值上涨”，既无数据支持，也无相关描述，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：龙涎香仍是香水制作中的重要成分。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “so ambergris was supplanted in the making of perfume by other materials, some natural and some synthetic in origin”，“supplanted” 意为 “取代”，说明龙涎香在香水制作中的地位已被其他物质取代，不再是 “重要成分”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：近期已为龙涎香发现新用途。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：全文仅提及龙涎香过去的用途（药用、香料、香水固定剂），以及后来被替代品取代，未提及 “近期发现新用途”，也无相关暗示，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

items.text:
（1）题目 10：In the 20th century, most of the world’s ambergris came from processing dead whales.
题目翻译：20 世纪，全球大部分龙涎香来自对死亡鲸体的加工。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “over 90 per cent of the annual worldwide total was acquired in this way, as a by-product of commercial whaling”，“this way” 指前文的 “the processing of the whales’ carcasses（鲸尸加工）”，且该情况发生在 19-20 世纪商业捕鲸时期，90% 以上的占比说明 “大部分” 龙涎香来自死亡鲸体加工，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（2）题目 11：The value of ambergris has increased recently.
题目翻译：龙涎香的价值近期有所上涨。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 6 仅提到龙涎香曾价值高昂，后因贸易禁令和替代品出现，其在香水制作中的地位下降，但未提及 “近期价值上涨”，既无数据支持，也无相关描述，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（3）题目 12：Ambergris remains an important ingredient in perfume.
题目翻译：龙涎香仍是香水制作中的重要成分。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “so ambergris was supplanted in the making of perfume by other materials, some natural and some synthetic in origin”，“supplanted” 意为 “取代”，说明龙涎香在香水制作中的地位已被其他物质取代，不再是 “重要成分”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（4）题目 13：New uses have recently been found for ambergris.
题目翻译：近期已为龙涎香发现新用途。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：全文仅提及龙涎香过去的用途（药用、香料、香水固定剂），以及后来被替代品取代，未提及 “近期发现新用途”，也无相关暗示，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
